revision.comment: 新しいページ：小墓古墳（奈良県天理市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小墓古墳 |画像 = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 15.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80.5m（推定復元85m以上）
高さ7.8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・木製品 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル = 小墓古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小墓古墳'''（おばかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から西に延びる丘陵の西端部に築造された古墳である。[[西乗鞍古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]などとともに杣之内古墳群のうちの一群を形成する。これまでに後円部北側が削平を受けているほか、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に墳丘東側の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける。墳丘は3段築成。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされており、幅12-13メートルを測る。周濠からは[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・盾形・靫形・人物形・馬形・鹿形・水鳥形・鶏形・家形埴輪など）のほか、「木製の埴輪」ともいうべき木製品（蓋形・盾形・刀形・翳形・鉾形木製品など）が多数検出されている{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。埴輪のうちでは、円柱を伴う高床式建物が特筆される。そのほかには木製の槽・槌・火切臼・耳杯形容器や、柱・杭なども検出されている。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでない。 この小墓古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半頃の[[6世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。付近では中期末葉-後期前半の古墳として西乗鞍古墳・東乗鞍古墳も知られ、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。 === 来歴 === * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）、測量調査（[[天理大学]]歴史研究会）{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第1次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1988年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第2次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1992年]]に概報刊行）。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：80.5メートル（推定復元85メートル以上） * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 高さ：7.8メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：46メートル{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}} ** 高さ：6.1メートル == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2009年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=泉武|year=2002|chapter=小墓古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=石田大輔|year=2014|chapter=小墓古墳の検討|title=[https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3280/?opkey=R157834405201772&idx=4 杣之内古墳群の研究]|publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} - リンクは天理大学学術情報リポジトリ。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=小墓古墳（付西乗鞍古墳立会）|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/750 天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 昭和61・62年度]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=小墓古墳（第2次）|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/740 天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 昭和63・平成元年度]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[西乗鞍古墳]] ** [[東乗鞍古墳]] * [[物部氏]] {{デフォルトソート:おはかこふん}} [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:物部氏|墓おはかこふん]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小墓古墳 |画像 = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 15.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80.5m（推定復元85m以上）
高さ7.8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・木製品 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル = 小墓古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小墓古墳'''（おばかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から西に延びる丘陵の西端部に築造された古墳である。[[西乗鞍古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]などとともに杣之内古墳群のうちの一群を形成する。これまでに後円部北側が削平を受けているほか、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に墳丘東側の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける。墳丘は3段築成。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされており、幅12-13メートルを測る。周濠からは[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・盾形・靫形・人物形・馬形・鹿形・水鳥形・鶏形・家形埴輪など）のほか、「木製の埴輪」ともいうべき木製品（蓋形・盾形・刀形・翳形・鉾形木製品など）が多数検出されている{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。埴輪のうちでは、円柱を伴う高床式建物が特筆される。そのほかには木製の槽・槌・火切臼・耳杯形容器や、柱・杭なども検出されている。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでない。 この小墓古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半頃の[[6世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。付近では中期末葉-後期前半の古墳として西乗鞍古墳・東乗鞍古墳も知られ、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。 === 来歴 === * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）、測量調査（[[天理大学]]歴史研究会）{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第1次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1988年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第2次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1992年]]に概報刊行）。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：80.5メートル（推定復元85メートル以上） * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 高さ：7.8メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：46メートル{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}} ** 高さ：6.1メートル == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2009年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=泉武|year=2002|chapter=小墓古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=杣之内古墳群研究会 |title=杣之内古墳群の研究 |publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会 |volume=1 |date=2014-03 |NCID=BB15553252 |id={{全国書誌番号|22415717}} |chapter=石田大輔、小墓古墳の検討 |url=https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3280/ |ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=泉武 |author2=松本洋明 |title=天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 |origdate=1988-03-30 |date=1988-03-30 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/750 |location=奈良県天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BN03007089 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.750 |volume=昭和61・62年度}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=天理市教育委員会 |title=天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 |origdate=1992-03-30 |date=1992-03-3 0|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/740 |location=奈良県天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BN03007089 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.740 |volume=昭和63・平成元年度}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=石田大輔 |author2=日野宏 |author3=藤原郁代 |title=物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群 |origdate=2021-07-14 |date=2021-07-14 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 |location=天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BC09207458 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.94674 }} == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[西乗鞍古墳]] ** [[東乗鞍古墳]] * [[物部氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:物部氏|墓おはかこふん]]

revision.comment: /* 構造 */ 構造→墳丘。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小墓古墳 |画像 = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 15.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80.5m（推定復元85m以上）
高さ7.8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・木製品 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル = 小墓古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小墓古墳'''（おばかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から西に延びる丘陵の西端部に築造された古墳である。[[西乗鞍古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]などとともに杣之内古墳群のうちの一群を形成する。これまでに後円部北側が削平を受けているほか、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に墳丘東側の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける。墳丘は3段築成。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされており、幅12-13メートルを測る。周濠からは[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・盾形・靫形・人物形・馬形・鹿形・水鳥形・鶏形・家形埴輪など）のほか、「木製の埴輪」ともいうべき木製品（蓋形・盾形・刀形・翳形・鉾形木製品など）が多数検出されている{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。埴輪のうちでは、円柱を伴う高床式建物が特筆される。そのほかには木製の槽・槌・火切臼・耳杯形容器や、柱・杭なども検出されている。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでない。 この小墓古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半頃の[[6世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。付近では中期末葉-後期前半の古墳として西乗鞍古墳・東乗鞍古墳も知られ、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。 === 来歴 === * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）、測量調査（[[天理大学]]歴史研究会）{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第1次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1988年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第2次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1992年]]に概報刊行）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：80.5メートル（推定復元85メートル以上） * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 高さ：7.8メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：46メートル{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}} ** 高さ：6.1メートル == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2009年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=泉武|year=2002|chapter=小墓古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=石田大輔 |title=杣之内古墳群の研究 |publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会 |volume=1 |date=2014-03 |NCID=BB15553252 |id={{全国書誌番号|22415717}} |chapter=小墓古墳の検討 |url=https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3280/ |ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=泉武 |author2=松本洋明 |title=天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 |origdate=1988-03-30 |date=1988-03-30 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/750 |location=奈良県天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BN03007089 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.750 |volume=昭和61・62年度}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=天理市教育委員会 |title=天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 |origdate=1992-03-30 |date=1992-03-3 0|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/740 |location=奈良県天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BN03007089 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.740 |volume=昭和63・平成元年度}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=石田大輔 |author2=日野宏 |author3=藤原郁代 |title=物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群 |origdate=2021-07-14 |date=2021-07-14 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 |location=天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BC09207458 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.94674 }} == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[西乗鞍古墳]] ** [[東乗鞍古墳]] * [[物部氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:物部氏|墓おはかこふん]]

墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 15.43 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長80.5m（推定復元85m以上）
高さ7.8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・木製品 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル = 小墓古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小墓古墳'''（おばかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から西に延びる丘陵の西端部に築造された古墳である。[[西乗鞍古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]などとともに杣之内古墳群のうちの一群を形成する。これまでに後円部北側が削平を受けているほか、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に墳丘東側の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける。墳丘は3段築成。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされており、幅12-13メートルを測る。周濠からは[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形・盾形・靫形・人物形・馬形・鹿形・水鳥形・鶏形・家形埴輪など）のほか、「木製の埴輪」ともいうべき木製品（蓋形・盾形・刀形・翳形・鉾形木製品など）が多数検出されている{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。埴輪のうちでは、円柱を伴う高床式建物が特筆される。そのほかには木製の槽・槌・火切臼・耳杯形容器や、柱・杭なども検出されている。埋葬施設は明らかでない。 この小墓古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半頃の[[6世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。。付近では中期末葉-後期前半の古墳として西乗鞍古墳・東乗鞍古墳も知られ、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）、測量調査（[[天理大学]]歴史研究会）{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第1次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1988年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、浄水場拡張に伴う発掘調査：第2次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[1992年]]に概報刊行）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：80.5メートル（推定復元85メートル以上） * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 高さ：7.8メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：46メートル{{Sfn|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}} ** 高さ：6.1メートル == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2009年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=小墓古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=泉武|year=2002|chapter=小墓古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|小墓古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=石田大輔 |title=杣之内古墳群の研究 |publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会 |volume=1 |date=2014-03 |NCID=BB15553252 |id={{全国書誌番号|22415717}} |chapter=小墓古墳の検討 |url=https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3280/ |ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=泉武 |author2=松本洋明 |title=天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 |origdate=1988-03-30 |date=1988-03-30 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/750 |location=奈良県天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BN03007089 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.750 |volume=昭和61・62年度}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=天理市教育委員会 |title=天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報 |origdate=1992-03-30 |date=1992-03-3 0|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/740 |location=奈良県天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BN03007089 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.740 |volume=昭和63・平成元年度}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=石田大輔 |author2=日野宏 |author3=藤原郁代 |title=物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群 |origdate=2021-07-14 |date=2021-07-14 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 |location=天理市川原城町605 |ncid=BC09207458 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.94674 }} == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[西乗鞍古墳]] ** [[東乗鞍古墳]] * [[物部氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:物部氏|墓おはかこふん]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Kobaka Kofun]] to [[Kohaka Kofun]]
revision.text: 小墓古墳

{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == Related Topics == * [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] ** [[Nishinorikura Kofun]] ** [[Higashinorikura Kofun]] * [[Mononobe clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おばかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]]

A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

revision.comment: Text replacement - "円筒埴輪}}" to "円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the burial mounds of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction * Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction * Width: 46 meters * Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
