Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

revision.comment: another replace
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun part_of]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun part_of|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun part_of. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like shape, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun part_of|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun part_of]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun part_of]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden artifacts |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designated as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} Artifacts recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden artifacts resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 15.43 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |label = Obaka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it forms part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been other_designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been other_designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been other_designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial_structure = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been other_designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:New3]]" to ""
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been other_designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound Structure == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been other_designation1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]
