revision.comment: Text replacement - "other_designation" to "Historic Site"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=draft}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=draft}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おはかこふん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Return the see also]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=draft}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] {{citation needed|おはかこふん}} {{citation needed|Obaka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=draft}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] {{citation needed|おはかこふん}} {{citation needed|Obaka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri City|qq=}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]
