revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85879644]] Wikidata name: Obaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}}

The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}}

[[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85879644]] Wikidata name: Obaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}}

It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85879644]] Wikidata name: Obaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85879644]] Wikidata name: Obaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85879644]] Wikidata name: Obaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85879644]] Wikidata name: Obaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
