[[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Zenpokoenfun" to "Zenpōkōenfun"
revision.text:
[[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.58761111111111|N|135.84411111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Obaka Kofun |image = [[File:Obaka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Somanouchi Town |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 15.43 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 38.77 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |size = Mound length: 80.5m (estimated restoration: over 85m)
Height: 7.8m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, wooden burial goods |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - SW |map_dot_label = Obaka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Obaka Kofun''' (小墓古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds comprising the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == Obaka Kofun was constructed at the western end of a ridge running east-west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. Together with the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. The northern side of the rear circular portion has been flattened, and excavations were conducted on the eastern side of the mound in 1987 and 1989. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular portion oriented to the southwest. It features a three-tier construction. A moat encircles the mound in a shield-like type, measuring 12–13 meters in width.{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} burial goods recovered from the moat include cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and shaped haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, waterfowl-shaped, rooster-shaped, and house-shaped haniwa. Wooden burial goods resembling haniwa, such as lid-shaped, shield-shaped, sword-shaped, scepter-shaped, and halberd-shaped items, have also been discovered. Among these, elevated-floor building models with cylindrical columns are particularly notable. Other finds include wooden troughs, mallets, fire-starting implements, ear-cup-shaped containers, and wooden poles and stakes. The burial chamber has not been identified. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q459939}}. Nishinorikura Kofun and Higashinorikura Kofun, located nearby and dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also hypothesized to be the Kofuns of {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} chiefs.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == Mound burial == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}} * Mound length: 80.5 meters (estimated restoration: over 85 meters) * Rear circular portion: Three-tier construction ** Height: 7.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: Three-tier construction ** Width: 46 meters ** Height: 6.1 meters File:小墓古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 盾持人.JPG|Shield-bearer haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} File:小墓古墳出土 埴輪 人物（男性）・甲冑・猪.JPG|Human figure (male) with armor and boar haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Izumi Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Obaka Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|ref={{Harvid|Obaka Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:小墓古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小墓古墳|version=100400383|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q85879644}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Obaka Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]
